metadata.graduation_notes: BS Computer Science can be completed in as few as 9 quarters (3 years). CMPSC 189A-B is an optional major-field elective—not required to graduate.

nodes.label: CMPSC 16
nodes.title: Problem Solving with Computers I
nodes.description: Introductory programming in Python: variables, control flow, functions, and problem decomposition for CS majors.

nodes.label: CMPSC 24
nodes.title: Problem Solving with Computers II
nodes.description: Intermediate Python: data structures, file I/O, testing, and software development practices building on CMPSC 16.

nodes.label: CMPSC 32
nodes.title: Object-Oriented Design & Implementation
nodes.description: Object-oriented programming in Java: classes, inheritance, polymorphism, and design patterns for scalable software.

nodes.label: CMPSC 40
nodes.title: Foundations of Computer Science
nodes.description: Discrete mathematics for CS: logic, proofs, sets, relations, combinatorics, and graph theory foundations.

nodes.label: CMPSC 64
nodes.title: Computer Organization & Logic Design
nodes.description: Digital logic, assembly language, memory hierarchy, and how hardware executes programs at the machine level.

nodes.label: PSTAT 120A
nodes.title: Probability & Statistics
nodes.description: Probability models, random variables, and statistical inference—prep for ML, algorithms, and data science tracks.

nodes.label: CMPSC 130A
nodes.title: Data Structures & Algorithms I
nodes.description: Core data structures (lists, trees, heaps, hash tables) and algorithm analysis with Big-O complexity.

nodes.label: CMPSC 130B
nodes.title: Data Structures & Algorithms II
nodes.description: Advanced algorithms: graph algorithms, dynamic programming, NP-completeness, and algorithm design techniques.

nodes.label: CMPSC 156
nodes.title: Advanced Applications Programming
nodes.description: Large-scale application development: web frameworks, databases, APIs, and team-based software engineering.

nodes.label: CMPSC 170
nodes.title: Operating Systems
nodes.description: Processes, threads, memory management, file systems, and concurrency in modern operating system design.

nodes.label: CMPSC 189A
nodes.title: Senior CS Capstone I
nodes.description: Optional 2-quarter industry capstone elective (Fall): team software project with industry sponsor—not required to graduate.

nodes.label: CMPSC 189B
nodes.title: Senior CS Capstone II
nodes.description: Optional capstone elective (Winter): complete and present the team project at CS Capstone Day.

nodes.label: ENGR 101
nodes.title: Ethics in Engineering
nodes.description: Professional ethics, social responsibility, and case studies in engineering and technology decision-making.

nodes.label: Software Engineer
nodes.title: Software Engineer
nodes.description: Build and ship production systems at startups and big tech—backend, frontend, and full-stack roles.

nodes.label: ML Engineer
nodes.title: Machine Learning Engineer
nodes.description: Develop models and ML pipelines for recommendation, NLP, computer vision, and data-driven products.

nodes.label: Security Engineer
nodes.title: Security Engineer
nodes.description: Protect systems through secure coding, penetration testing, and security architecture in industry or research.

nodes.label: Grad School
nodes.title: Graduate School (CS PhD / MS)
nodes.description: Research-focused path in algorithms, systems, AI, or theory via MS/PhD programs and faculty-led projects.
